summary: n8n-nodes-pwn exfiltrates host recon and /root contents to hardcoded IP on node load
details: Pwn.node.js executes top-level code when the n8n node is loaded that runs a series of host-reconnaissance shell commands via execSync (id, hostname, sudo -l, find / -perm -4000, ls -la /root, cat /root/*.txt, cat /root/flag*, env, find / -iname "*flag*") and concatenates their output. The combined output is written to /tmp/.pwnout2 and POSTed via http.request to the hardcoded endpoint http://10.0.0.145:8000/rce-out2. The exfiltrated data includes installer environment variables in bulk and the contents of files under /root, sent to a non-first-party destination the installer did not configure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
